title: monarda fistulosa var. menthifolia
intro: Monarda fistulosa var. menthifolia, also known as the mint-leaved beebalm, is a plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, characterized by its mint-scented leaves and pink, purple, or white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Monarda fistulosa var. menthifolia include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, promote relaxation, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, or topical creams, allowing for various forms of administration and utilization of its medicinal properties.
